task_completion.description: Create the recovery script file in the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, terminal command (like touch or nano), or file manager showing a file named 'recovery_script.sql' located in '/home/ga/Documents/scripts/'.

task_completion.description: Write the SQL commands to restore Invoices and InvoiceItems
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the contents of the SQL script showing commands to ATTACH the backup database and INSERT data into the Invoices and InvoiceItems tables selecting from the backup.

task_completion.description: Restore the 2009 Invoices to the production database
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal or DB GUI output showing the successful execution of the INSERT command for 2009 invoices, or a SELECT query result showing exactly 83 invoices for the year 2009 in the production DB.

task_completion.description: Restore the 2009 InvoiceItems to the production database
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal or DB GUI output showing the successful execution of the INSERT command for 2009 invoice items, or a SELECT query result showing exactly 454 invoice items associated with 2009 invoices.

task_completion.description: Ensure the existing 2025 data remains intact
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a SELECT query result on the production database showing that invoices from the year 2025 (exactly 5 invoices) are still present after the recovery operation.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual database using a valid client
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent used a real database client (e.g., sqlite3 CLI, DBeaver, DB Browser for SQLite) to execute the queries, rather than just mocking the output in a text file.

integrity.description: Agent used dynamic SQL rather than hardcoding values
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the SQL script uses 'INSERT INTO ... SELECT ... FROM backup' (or similar logic), rather than manually hardcoding all 83 invoices and 454 items in raw INSERT statements.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely saved the recovery script to disk
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the recovery script was actually saved to the file system (e.g., seeing a successful save in a text editor or a valid echo/cat command), not just typed into a temporary prompt.
